name: Slate Edge
effective_cost: Free — no annual fee, but no longer available to new applicants

verdict.text: Discontinued for new applicants as of Feb 2026 — existing holders can keep it, but new credit-builders should look at Freedom Rise or the relaunched Chase Slate instead

earn_note: Slate Edge never earned cash back or points — it's built entirely around a 0% intro APR and an ongoing APR-reduction mechanic, not rewards. If you want cash back on a starter/credit-building Chase card, use Freedom Rise instead.

points.note: This card has no points or cash-back program of any kind. Its entire value proposition is interest-rate management: a 0% intro APR window plus an ongoing 2% APR reduction each account anniversary (down to a floor rate) for cardholders who spend $1,000/year and pay on time.

transfer_partners.highlight: No rewards currency exists on this card, so there is nothing to transfer.

protection_note: Minimal by design — this is a credit-building/APR-management card, not a travel or rewards card. No trip cancellation, baggage, rental car, or cell phone coverage.
rental_note: No rental car coverage at all — use a different card for rentals.

services.name: 0% intro APR
services.detail: 0% for 18 months on purchases and balance transfers, then a variable ~22–30% ongoing rate.

services.name: Automatic APR reduction
services.detail: Cardholders who spend at least $1,000 and pay on time in an account year get an automatic 2-percentage-point APR cut at each account anniversary, potentially down to a floor around 18–19%.

services.name: Automatic credit line reviews
services.detail: Chase automatically checks the account for a credit line increase roughly every 6 months.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee likely applies
services.detail: Not listed among Chase's no-foreign-transaction-fee cards; treat it as carrying the standard fee abroad.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards
additional_cards.note: No distinct authorized-user program is marketed for this card, and it's no longer accepting new applicants of any kind as of February 2026.

timeline.text: Chase stops accepting new Slate Edge applications and reintroduces a plain Chase Slate card (0% intro APR for 21 months on balance transfers, but without the ongoing APR-reduction feature) as its replacement for new applicants.

Chase introduces Slate Edge as a no-annual-fee card built around an interest rate that could go down over time with responsible use, rather than around rewards. Launched with a 12-month 0% intro APR and a $100 statement credit for spending $500 in the first 6 months.
